narrative: I'm sorry to share that your law school application was rejected because our predictive model estimated a 42% chance you'll fail the bar exam on your first try, based on patterns from thousands of past students. This prediction draws from key details in your profile, like your undergraduate GPA of 2.20, LSAT score of 31, Year 1 and Year 3 grade decile rankings of 4 each—both below the typical student's 5.7 and 5.5—and your lower-middle family income bracket. You're also a full-time Asian female applicant, which aligns with many applicants but stands out in a dataset where most are white males from higher income groups. The model's decision hinged most heavily on your Year 3 grade decile, as being in the lower half of your class there strongly nudged the prediction toward expecting bar struggles, more than any other factor. Your LSAT score came in second, pulling in the same concerning direction since it's notably below the average of 36.7. Your Year 1 grade decile ranked third in influence, again signaling early academic challenges compared to peers. Family income followed closely, with lower-middle status contributing to the risk assessment in a way that mirrors trends among students from similar backgrounds. Your racial background as Asian had a similar but slightly lesser push toward the failure prediction, given its underrepresentation in the successful group. Undergraduate GPA rounded out the top influencers, with your 2.20 well under the 3.21 average, reinforcing those worries. Being full-time played a minor role in the same direction, while your gender as female offered a small counterbalance, gently favoring a passing prediction like it does for many women in the data. Overall, your academic markers sit below what's typical for students who pass the bar first time, tipping the scales despite some neutral demographics. To boost your future odds, focus on realistic steps like intensive test prep to retake and raise your LSAT, seeking academic support to build stronger grades in any further coursework, and exploring resources tailored for bar success from lower-income backgrounds. With targeted effort in these areas, you could shift patterns that held back this prediction and strengthen your path forward. Keep pushing—you have the determination to rewrite this story.
